notes: all combat numbers are simulator output on unverified constants
checkpoint levels are provisional (no level timeline in the match feed)
evidence deltas carry finished-inventory survivorship bias

coachLine: Sim-optimal for skirmish uptime and extended fights: first spike lands around minute 12 (Spectral Schematics), third item by minute 25. Take Demiurge: +13.5% on 20-second fights at minute 15.

all.name: Marrow
all.note: Marrow (Major): missing-health-dependent omnivamp; no sustain-uptime model yet

all.name: Nihil
all.note: Nihil (Major): accrual rate depends on combat uptime (how long Doom persists per fight), which the closed-form sim cannot observe

all.name: Aion
all.note: Aion (Major): economy effect; valuable but outside combat objectives (revisit with gold curves)

all.name: Exarch
all.note: Exarch (Major): buffs the HEAL TARGET (usually an ally) — team-side value outside the single-hero sim; self-heal uptime depends on heal cadence vs the 4s window

metaBuilds.title: Magical Bruiser (Mixed-Def)
metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for magical survivability, online around minute 24 — about 5,858 effective HP vs magical in the sim.

metaBuilds.title: Magical DPS Carry
metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for sustained auto DPS, online around minute 24 — about 221 auto-attack DPS in the sim.

metaBuilds.title: Magical Bruiser (Physical-Def)
metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for physical survivability, online around minute 24 — about 8,577 effective HP vs physical in the sim.

matchups.gameplan: Your window is early: force trades before their core completes, and do not coin-flip fights past the crossover. Steel deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Void Helm instead of Oblivion Crown: their 3s all-in loses 29% of its bite; you give up 20% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: You hold the kill-threat edge wherever one exists: play front-foot, but respect jungle attention. Grux deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Warden's Faith instead of Oblivion Crown: their 3s all-in loses 33% of its bite; you give up 20% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: You hold the kill-threat edge wherever one exists: play front-foot, but respect jungle attention. Bayle deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Warden's Faith instead of Oblivion Crown: their 3s all-in loses 33% of its bite; you give up 20% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: Your window is early: force trades before their core completes, and do not coin-flip fights past the crossover. Greystone deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Warden's Faith instead of Oblivion Crown: their 3s all-in loses 37% of its bite; you give up 20% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: They out-threaten you whenever the lane is not even: treat this as a farm-and-team lane, not a duel. Zarus's main threat is Coliseum; bait it before you commit, and armor softens the trade.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Warden's Faith instead of Oblivion Crown: their 3s all-in loses 33% of its bite; you give up 20% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: Your window is early: force trades before their core completes, and do not coin-flip fights past the crossover. Terra ramps on auto-attacks — pressure them before they itemize, and mixed resist blunts them later.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Raiment of Renewal instead of Oblivion Crown: their 3s all-in loses 24% of its bite; you give up 20% of your damage.

laneSteer.provenance: “Hypothermia” ⇒ on-hit; the sim models this augment — field offlane 50.6% over 512 games.

metaBuilds.title: Magical Bruiser (Mixed-Def)
metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for magical survivability, online around minute 24 — about 5,858 effective HP vs magical in the sim.

metaBuilds.title: Magical DPS Carry
metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for sustained auto DPS, online around minute 24 — about 221 auto-attack DPS in the sim.

metaBuilds.title: Magical Bruiser (Physical-Def)
metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for physical survivability, online around minute 24 — about 8,577 effective HP vs physical in the sim.

matchups.gameplan: You hold the kill-threat edge wherever one exists: play front-foot, but respect jungle attention. Khaimera's main threat is Cull; bait it before you commit, and armor softens the trade.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Warden's Faith instead of Wraith Leggings: their 3s all-in loses 32% of its bite; you give up 21% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: You hold the kill-threat edge wherever one exists: play front-foot, but respect jungle attention. Serath's main threat is Chastise; bait it before you commit, and armor softens the trade.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Warden's Faith instead of Wraith Leggings: their 3s all-in loses 33% of its bite; you give up 22% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: You hold the kill-threat edge wherever one exists: play front-foot, but respect jungle attention. Boris deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Raiment of Renewal instead of Wraith Leggings: their 3s all-in loses 23% of its bite; you give up 19% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: You hold the kill-threat edge wherever one exists: play front-foot, but respect jungle attention. Yin's main threat is Backlash; bait it before you commit, and armor softens the trade.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Warden's Faith instead of Wraith Leggings: their 3s all-in loses 33% of its bite; you give up 22% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: Your kill threat peaks early-to-mid: cash it in before their later items level the exchange. Kwang ramps on auto-attacks — pressure them before they itemize, and mixed resist blunts them later.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Void Helm instead of Wraith Leggings: their 3s all-in loses 28% of its bite; you give up 20% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: You hold the kill-threat edge wherever one exists: play front-foot, but respect jungle attention. Kallari's main threat is Guillotine; bait it before you commit, and armor softens the trade.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Warden's Faith instead of Wraith Leggings: their 3s all-in loses 33% of its bite; you give up 21% of your damage.

laneSteer.provenance: “Glacial Thorns” ⇒ ability-burst; the sim models this augment — field jungle 53.9% over 841 games.

flags: crests and consumables not yet in the build model; augments with tractable mechanics are simulated, the rest are listed unmodeled
